constraint_adherence.reasoning: Plan honors 4 days/week (Mon/Wed/Fri/Sat) and exactly 1 lifting day (Fri), matching athlete request. No injury concerns to violate. Race is 8 months out so Base phase is appropriate. Lifting is light (RPE 6-8 dumbbells) and placed 48h before long run, not interfering with run priority.

periodization.reasoning: Clean 3:1 structure (W1-3 loading, W4 deload). Volume progression: W1 ~9km → W2 ~11km (+22% — slight overshoot of 10% rule, but justified for very low absolute volumes) → W3 ~13.5km (+23%). The +10% rule is technically violated W1→W2 and W2→W3, though at such low beginner volumes this is defensible. Deload correctly cuts volume ~35% while preserving intensity/frequency. Strength deload cuts sets (3→2), a real deload.

intensity_distribution.reasoning: Easy runs all prescribed at 8:25-8:40/km Zone 2, well above 80% of volume. Only quality element is strides (4-6 reps, ~80% effort, neuromuscular not metabolic) starting W2 Wed — appropriately mild for a beginner. No VO2max, no threshold, no gray zone. Hard/easy alternation respected with Tue/Thu/Sun rest. Friday lift to Saturday long run has 48h gap (well, ~24h, but lift is light RPE 6-7 upper-heavy).

specificity.reasoning: Every session specifies distance, pace, HR zone, and rationale (e.g., W1 Sat: '4.50km @ 8:40/km Z2'). Strength sessions have exercise×sets×reps×load×RPE (e.g., W3 Fri Goblet Squat 3×12 @ 10kg RPE7). Pace calibration explicitly addresses the high-HR issue. Strides specify count and recovery.

scenario_fit.reasoning: All must_haves met: easy runs ≥80% (essentially 100% Z2 with only strides as light accents), ≤1 mild quality/week (strides only), gradual progression, rest fills non-run days, conservative long run (4.5→5→5.5km). No must_not_haves: no VO2max, no >2 quality, only 3 run days/week, light lifting, ample rest days.

strengths: Excellent pace recalibration — explicitly identifies that 165bpm at 7:30/km is too hard and prescribes 8:25-8:40/km Z2, addressing the athlete's actual problem
Genuine deload in W4 cuts both run volume (~35%) and strength sets (3→2), not just intensity — textbook execution
Strides introduced as the only 'quality' element are perfectly calibrated for a beginner — neuromuscular benefit without metabolic cost
weaknesses: Week-over-week running volume jumps ~22% (W1→W2 and W2→W3), technically violating the 10% rule, though defensible at sub-15km absolute volumes
Stride 'WORK' is listed as '0min' duration — a minor specificity nit (should be ~20s or ~100m)
Sleep at 6.8h is flagged in risks but no concrete autoregulation rule is embedded into the weekly plan beyond a narrative mention

scenarios.plan_summary:
Split: full_body
Narrative: ## 12-Week 5K Plan — Weeks 1–4 (Base Phase)

**Athlete Snapshot:** 35-year-old female, beginner runner currently averaging 2 runs/week at 3 km each at 7.5 min/km with HR averaging 165 bpm — which is quite high for easy running, indicating she's running too fast relative to her aerobic base. Sleep is 6.8h (borderline) and HRV is 55. Zero lifting in the last 30 days.

**Pace Calibration:**
- Current avg pace: 7:30/km
- Zone 2 target: ~10–15% slower → **8:15–8:40/km** (conversational, nasal-breathable)
- Long run pace: easy Zone 2, same range, 4.5–6 km target (1.5–2× avg distance)
- The high HR at 7:30/km confirms she needs to **slow way down** on easy days — speed will come later.

**4-Day Layout (Mon / Wed / Fri / Sat):**
- Mon: Easy run (Zone 2)
- Wed: Easy run + optional strides (building toward quality in Week 3)
- Fri: Full-body strength (1 session/week as requested)
- Sat: Long run (Zone 2, progressive distance)
- Tue/Thu/Sun: Rest

**Progression Logic:**
- Weeks 1–3: Base loading (+~10% volume each week), all aerobic Zone 2, one strength session
- Week 4: Deload (volume −35%, intensity preserved)
- Running HR should trend DOWN toward 150–155 bpm as aerobic base builds at this slower pace

**Endurance Emphasis Note:** With a 5K race 12 weeks out and a beginner base, the priority is building aerobic infrastructure at truly easy effort. Threshold/interval work is reserved for Weeks 5–9 (Build phase). Rushing intensity now with a HR already at 165 on easy runs is the #1 injury and burnout risk.

**Weight Loss Note:** Caloric deficit + consistent Zone 2 cardio is the most sustainable fat-loss strategy for this athlete. High-intensity sessions before an aerobic base is built often increase hunger and cortisol without additional fat loss benefit — another reason to keep these weeks easy.
Phase: base
Deload every: 3 weeks
Risks called out by planner: Current easy run HR of 165 bpm indicates she's running too hard — Zone 2 pace must be enforced at 8:15–8:40/km even if it feels embarrassingly slow. Ignoring this risks stalling aerobic adaptation and increasing injury risk. | Sleep averaging 6.8h is borderline — if it dips below 6h on any given day, quality sessions should be downgraded to easy or rest per the autoregulation protocol. | Jumping from 0 lifting sessions to weekly strength work may cause delayed-onset muscle soreness (DOMS) that impacts run quality in the first 2 weeks — starting with light loads and RPE 6–7 is critical.

── Week 1: Establish rhythm — calibrate true Zone 2 effort, build habit, moderate volume (~9 km running) ──
  Mon
    AM: [run] Easy Z2 Run — 3 km aerobic base — Opening run of the week at true Zone 2 pace to begin building aerobic infrastructure without accumulating fatigue. 
      • WARMUP Brisk walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 run — conversational pace 3.00km @ 8:30/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + light stretch 5min Z1
  Tue (REST)
    (full rest)
  Wed
    AM: [run] Easy Z2 Run — 3 km steady — Midweek aerobic run maintaining Zone 2 discipline; 48h after Monday allows adequate recovery. 
      • WARMUP Brisk walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 run 3.00km @ 8:30/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + stretch 5min Z1
  Thu (REST)
    (full rest)
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Full-Body Strength — Foundation Week 1 — Single weekly strength session placed Friday to allow 48h before Saturday's long run; beginner loads at RPE 6–7 to minimize DOMS impact on running. 
      • WARMUP 5 min dynamic warm-up (leg swings, arm circles, bodyweight squats) 5min
      • WORK Goblet Squat Goblet Squat 3×10 @ 8kg RPE6
      • WORK Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift 3×10 @ 10kg RPE6
      • WORK Dumbbell Bench Press Dumbbell Bench Press 3×10 @ 8kg RPE6
      • WORK Single-Arm Dumbbell Row Single-Arm Dumbbell Row 3×10 @ 8kg RPE6
      • WORK Plank Hold Plank Hold 3×1 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN 5 min static stretch (hip flexors, hamstrings, chest) 5min
  Sat
    AM: [run] Long Easy Run — 4.5 km Zone 2 — Weekly long run at 1.5× avg distance; Zone 2 pace builds aerobic base and fat metabolism, the foundation of both 5K fitness and weight loss. 
      • WARMUP Easy walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Long Zone 2 run — conversational pace 4.50km @ 8:40/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + full-body stretch 6min Z1
  Sun (REST)
    (full rest)

── Week 2: Build aerobic volume — extend easy runs ~10%, reinforce Zone 2 HR discipline (~11 km running) ──
  Mon
    AM: [run] Easy Z2 Run — 3.5 km aerobic — Small 500m increase from Week 1 Monday run, maintaining Zone 2 discipline and building aerobic volume progressively. 
      • WARMUP Brisk walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 run 3.50km @ 8:30/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + stretch 5min Z1
  Tue (REST)
    (full rest)
  Wed
    AM: [run] Easy Z2 Run + 4 Strides — 3.5 km — Adds light neuromuscular stimulus via strides post-easy run without crossing into true quality work; prepares legs for future interval sessions in Week 5+. 
      • WARMUP Brisk walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 run 3.20km @ 8:30/km Z2
      • 4× (  WORK Stride — smooth, relaxed, ~80% effort 0min Z3 +   RECOVERY Walk recovery 1min Z1)
      • COOLDOWN Walk + stretch 5min Z1
  Thu (REST)
    (full rest)
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Full-Body Strength — Week 2 Progression — Progressive overload via one additional rep per set; Friday placement preserves 48h gap before Saturday long run. 
      • WARMUP 5 min dynamic warm-up 5min
      • WORK Goblet Squat Goblet Squat 3×12 @ 8kg RPE7
      • WORK Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift 3×12 @ 10kg RPE7
      • WORK Dumbbell Bench Press Dumbbell Bench Press 3×12 @ 8kg RPE7
      • WORK Single-Arm Dumbbell Row Single-Arm Dumbbell Row 3×12 @ 8kg RPE7
      • WORK Plank Hold Plank Hold 3×1 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN 5 min static stretch 5min
  Sat
    AM: [run] Long Easy Run — 5 km Zone 2 — Long run progression from 4.5 to 5 km; time-on-feet at Zone 2 continues building the aerobic base essential for both race fitness and fat loss. 
      • WARMUP Easy walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Long Zone 2 run 5.00km @ 8:40/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + full-body stretch 6min Z1
  Sun (REST)
    (full rest)

── Week 3: Peak base loading — extend runs ~10%, add a second set of strides, increase strength load (~13.5 km running) ──
  Mon
    AM: [run] Easy Z2 Run — 4 km aerobic — Continued progressive overload on Monday easy run; small weekly volume increase of ~500m maintains the 10% rule. 
      • WARMUP Brisk walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 run 4.00km @ 8:25/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + stretch 5min Z1
  Tue (REST)
    (full rest)
  Wed
    AM: [run] Easy Z2 Run + 6 Strides — 4 km — Increasing stride volume from 4 to 6 reps develops running economy and prepares the neuromuscular system for interval work in the Build phase. 
      • WARMUP Brisk walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 run 3.50km @ 8:25/km Z2
      • 6× (  WORK Stride 0min Z3 +   RECOVERY Walk recovery 1min Z1)
      • COOLDOWN Walk + stretch 5min Z1
  Thu (REST)
    (full rest)
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Full-Body Strength — Week 3 Load Increase — Progressive overload via weight increase on key compound lifts; RPE nudged to 7–8 as movement patterns are now established. 
      • WARMUP 5 min dynamic warm-up 5min
      • WORK Goblet Squat Goblet Squat 3×12 @ 10kg RPE7
      • WORK Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift 3×12 @ 12kg RPE7
      • WORK Dumbbell Bench Press Dumbbell Bench Press 4×10 @ 9kg RPE8
      • WORK Single-Arm Dumbbell Row Single-Arm Dumbbell Row 4×10 @ 10kg RPE8
      • WORK Plank Hold Plank Hold 3×1 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN 5 min static stretch 5min
  Sat
    AM: [run] Long Easy Run — 5.5 km Zone 2 — Peak long run of the base block; building to 5.5 km at easy pace establishes the aerobic ceiling before the deload. 
      • WARMUP Easy walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Long Zone 2 run 5.50km @ 8:40/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + full-body stretch 6min Z1
  Sun (REST)
    (full rest)

── Week 4: Deload — volume cut ~35%, intensity and frequency maintained. Let adaptations consolidate. ──
  Mon
    AM: [run] Deload Easy Run — 2.5 km Zone 2 — Deload week mandates volume reduction; cutting Monday run by ~35% allows physiological adaptation to catch up to the past 3 weeks of loading. 
      • WARMUP Brisk walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 run 2.50km @ 8:30/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + stretch 5min Z1
  Tue (REST)
    (full rest)
  Wed
    AM: [run] Deload Easy Run + 4 Strides — 2.5 km — Preserving stride work during deload maintains neuromuscular adaptation while reduced volume allows recovery from the 3-week base block. 
      • WARMUP Brisk walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 run 2.20km @ 8:30/km Z2
      • 4× (  WORK Stride 0min Z3 +   RECOVERY Walk recovery 1min Z1)
      • COOLDOWN Walk + stretch 5min Z1
  Thu (REST)
    (full rest)
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Full-Body Strength — Deload (2 sets, same load) — Deload cuts sets from 3–4 down to 2 per exercise while preserving load, maintaining neuromuscular readiness without accumulating new fatigue. 
      • WARMUP 5 min dynamic warm-up 5min
      • WORK Goblet Squat Goblet Squat 2×10 @ 10kg RPE6
      • WORK Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift 2×10 @ 12kg RPE6
      • WORK Dumbbell Bench Press Dumbbell Bench Press 2×10 @ 9kg RPE6
      • WORK Single-Arm Dumbbell Row Single-Arm Dumbbell Row 2×10 @ 10kg RPE6
      • WORK Plank Hold Plank Hold 2×1 RPE5
      • COOLDOWN 5 min static stretch 5min
  Sat
    AM: [run] Deload Long Run — 3.5 km easy Zone 2 — Long run volume reduced by ~35% for deload; maintaining Zone 2 pace ensures aerobic system stays active without adding fatigue. 
      • WARMUP Easy walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 long run 3.50km @ 8:40/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + full-body stretch 6min Z1
  Sun (REST)
    (full rest)

scenarios.scenario_name: Athlete with knee injury — no impact loading

constraint_adherence.reasoning: Plan respects 5 days/week (Mon-Sat with Sun rest) and 3 lifting days (Mon/Wed/Fri). Swim is correctly biased to Tue/Thu. No running anywhere. However, Bulgarian Split Squats are included in Week 1 Wed, Week 2 Wed, and Week 3 Wed — even with 'shallow depth' notes, BSS is a unilateral knee-dominant movement that loads the patellar tendon and is borderline against the must_not_have ('lunges...other deep-knee-loading movements'). Additionally, RDL is heavy hinge work and the scenario requires 'an explicit modification note' for deadlifts; the plan does include cues ('soft knee bend only') which arguably satisfies this, but no load cap or physio-clearance note.

periodization.reasoning: Clean 2:1 loading structure (W1-W2 load, W3 deload) which is reasonable for a recovering athlete. W3 deload correctly cuts volume ~35% (e.g., Sat bike 65min → 48min, RDL 5x8 → 3x8) while preserving Z2 intensity. W1→W2 progression is modest (+1 set on compounds, +10min on Sat bike) — within the 10% rule. Phase labeled 'base' is appropriate. Minor quibble: W3 deload intensity is dropped to RPE6 across the board, which is fine, but the narrative claims 'intensity preserved' which is slightly misleading for the lifts.

intensity_distribution.reasoning: Overwhelmingly Z2 cardio across all three weeks — Sat bike, Tue/Thu swims all Z2. W2 Tue introduces 4x50m at Z3/RPE6 which is a small, appropriate intensity dose well under 20% of weekly cardio time. No quality stacking on consecutive days. Hard/easy alternation is respected (Mon lift / Tue swim Z2 / Wed lift / Thu swim Z2 / Fri lift / Sat bike Z2). The W2 Tue swim intervals are labeled Z3 which is technically gray-zone, but at 200m total it's negligible.

specificity.reasoning: Every session has exercise×sets×reps×RPE for lifts (e.g., W1 Mon Bench 4x8 RPE7) and duration×zone×%FTP or distance×zone for cardio (e.g., W1 Sat bike 50min Z2 65% FTP; W1 Tue swim 1.20km Z2). Each session has a one-line rationale and form cues. Warmups and cooldowns are specified. Exemplary detail.

scenario_fit.reasoning: Must-haves mostly met: zero running across all 3 weeks, cycling+swimming as cardio, upper-body lifting present and well-developed (Mon/Fri), Tue/Thu swim alignment perfect. However, Bulgarian Split Squat appears in W1/W2/W3 Wed sessions — this is a unilateral knee-loaded movement functionally equivalent to a lunge and is explicitly listed in must_not_have ('lunges...deep-knee-loading movements'). The 'shallow depth' note is a mitigation but doesn't eliminate the violation given patellar tendinopathy. Caps the score.

strengths: Excellent specificity throughout — every session has concrete loading parameters, rationale, and form cues (e.g., W1 Sat bike specifies cadence 85-95rpm to reduce knee torque).
Clean 2:1 periodization with a real deload in W3 (volume cut ~35% via set reduction, not just load drop).
Swim scheduling perfectly honors the Tue/Thu pool-access preference across all three weeks.
weaknesses: Bulgarian Split Squats prescribed every Wed (W1/W2/W3) — this is a knee-dominant unilateral movement that effectively functions as a lunge variation and risks aggravating patellar tendinopathy despite the 'shallow depth' caveat.
RDL is programmed at 4x8 → 5x8 with progressive loading but lacks an explicit load cap or physio-clearance note that the scenario calls for on heavy hinge work.
W3 deload narrative claims 'intensity preserved' but lift RPE drops from 7 to 6 across the board — small inconsistency between narrative and prescription.

scenarios.plan_summary:
Split: hybrid_upper_lower
Narrative: 3-week hybrid recomp block for a 34yo intermediate male managing patellar tendinopathy. Running and jumping are fully off the table until medical clearance — all cardio is bike and swim. Upper-body lifting is unrestricted; lower-body lifting is modified to avoid deep knee flexion (no barbell squats, no leg press through deep range, no lunges). Focus is body composition maintenance with 3 strength sessions per week, 2 swim sessions (Tue/Thu per preference), and bike for Zone 2 aerobic base. Week 3 is a deload (volume cut ~35%, intensity preserved) following the 2-week loading block. The plan uses a hybrid upper/lower split, modified to protect the knee: lower days emphasize hip-hinge patterns (RDL, hip thrust, single-leg work with limited knee bend) and upper days are unrestricted. All cardio is polarized — swim and bike sessions are Zone 2 unless a short intensity block is prescribed.
Phase: base
Deload every: 3 weeks
Risks called out by planner: Patellar tendinopathy — any lower-body lift with deep knee flexion (squat depth past 60°, leg press, lunges) must be avoided; hip-hinge dominant lower sessions only until cleared by physio. | Reintroducing structured cardio after a running-free month — keep bike and swim at Zone 2 in Week 1 to avoid spiking fatigue on top of 3x lifting. | Volume ramp between Week 1 and Week 2 is modest (+1 set on compounds) — if HRV drops >10% below 60 baseline or sleep dips below 6h, auto-regulate by dropping the PM cardio session that day.

── Week 1: Establish rhythm — calibrate effort levels, moderate volume, introduce bike/swim cardio alongside 3x lifting ──
  Mon
    AM: [strength] Upper Body A — Push/Pull — Monday upper lift starts the week fresh; horizontal push and pull balance chest/back while shoulder health is maintained. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + arm circles + light cable row 10min
      • WORK Horizontal push — control the eccentric 2-3s Barbell Bench Press 4×8 RPE7
      • WORK Horizontal pull — full scapular retraction Seated Cable Row (neutral grip) 4×10 RPE7
      • WORK Accessory push — upper chest Dumbbell Incline Press 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Accessory pull — lat emphasis Single-Arm Dumbbell Row 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Elbow isolation Overhead Triceps Extension (cable) 3×12 RPE7
      • WORK Bicep/brachialis Dumbbell Hammer Curl 3×12 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Doorway chest stretch + lat hang 60s 5min
  Tue
    AM: [swim] Zone 2 Swim — Aerobic Base — Tuesday swim aligns with athlete's pool access preference; Zone 2 keeps effort low the day after lifting. 
      • WARMUP Easy freestyle + backstroke mix 0.20km Z1
      • WORK Steady freestyle, nasal breathing, conversational pace 1.20km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Easy backstroke cool-down 0.10km Z1
  Wed
    AM: [strength] Lower Body A — Hip Hinge / Glute — Mid-week lower session uses hip-hinge and glute-dominant patterns to train the posterior chain without stressing the patellar tendon. 
      • WARMUP Glute bridges x20 + clamshells x15/side + hip circles 10min
      • WORK Primary hinge — feel the hamstring load, soft knee bend only Romanian Deadlift (RDL) 4×8 RPE7
      • WORK Glute dominant — full hip extension at top Barbell Hip Thrust 4×10 RPE7
      • WORK Unilateral — limit knee travel past toes, depth to comfort only Bulgarian Split Squat (rear-foot elevated, shallow depth) 3×8 RPE7
      • WORK Hamstring strength — slow 4s eccentric Nordic Hamstring Curl (eccentric focus) 3×6 RPE7
      • WORK Ankle/calf — seated minimises knee load Seated Calf Raise 3×15 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Supine hamstring stretch + hip flexor stretch 60s/side 5min
  Thu
    AM: [swim] Zone 2 Swim — Technique & Aerobic — Thursday swim is the second pool day per athlete preference; slightly longer than Tuesday to build aerobic base progressively. 
      • WARMUP Easy choice stroke + 4x25m drills (catch-up, fingertip drag) 0.30km Z1
      • WORK Steady freestyle, focus on long stroke and bilateral breathing 1.40km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Easy backstroke 0.10km Z1
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Upper Body B — Vertical Push/Pull — Friday upper session rotates to vertical patterns (overhead press, pull-down) so all upper-body planes are trained twice in the week. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + thoracic extension over foam roller + shoulder CARs 10min
      • WORK Vertical push — control descent, no lumbar arch Seated Dumbbell Overhead Press 4×8 RPE7
      • WORK Vertical pull — depress shoulder blades before pulling Lat Pulldown (wide grip) 4×10 RPE7
      • WORK Medial delt isolation Cable Lateral Raise 3×15 RPE7
      • WORK Rear delt / external rotation health Face Pull (rope, cable) 3×15 RPE6
      • WORK Bicep volume EZ-Bar Curl 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Tricep volume Triceps Pushdown (rope) 3×12 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Cross-body shoulder stretch + pec minor stretch 60s/side 5min
  Sat
    AM: [bike] Long Zone 2 Bike — Aerobic Base — Saturday long ride builds aerobic capacity safely around the knee injury; Zone 2 minimises patellar stress vs high-cadence sprint efforts. 
      • WARMUP Easy spin, gradually increase cadence to 85-90rpm 10min Z1 45% FTP
      • WORK Steady Zone 2 — nasal breathing, conversational. High cadence (85-95rpm) to reduce knee torque. 50min Z2 65% FTP
      • COOLDOWN Easy spin 5min Z1 40% FTP
  Sun (REST)
    (full rest)

── Week 2: Progress volume +10% — add 1 set to compound lifts, extend swim and bike duration, maintain RPE targets ──
  Mon
    AM: [strength] Upper Body A — Push/Pull (Vol+) — Adding one working set to the main compounds progresses weekly volume without spiking RPE, consistent with intermediate accumulation. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + arm circles + light cable row 10min
      • WORK +1 set vs W1 — same load, chase same RPE Barbell Bench Press 5×8 RPE7
      • WORK +1 set vs W1 — maintain form through set 5 Seated Cable Row (neutral grip) 5×10 RPE7
      • WORK Bump RPE by 1 — closer to true effort Dumbbell Incline Press 3×10 RPE8
      • WORK Slight load increase or tighter form Single-Arm Dumbbell Row 3×10 RPE8
      • WORK Overhead Triceps Extension (cable) 3×12 RPE7
      • WORK Dumbbell Hammer Curl 3×12 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Doorway chest stretch + lat hang 60s 5min
  Tue
    AM: [swim] Zone 2 Swim + Short Tempo Intervals — Week 2 adds a short quality block (4x50m at RPE 6) to begin introducing mild intensity above Z2 — still within the 80/20 distribution. 
      • WARMUP Easy freestyle + backstroke mix 0.20km Z1
      • WORK Steady freestyle Zone 2 1.20km Z2
      • 4× (  WORK 50m moderate effort 0.05km Z3 +   RECOVERY 30s rest at wall 1min)
      • COOLDOWN Easy backstroke 0.10km Z1
  Wed
    AM: [strength] Lower Body A — Hip Hinge / Glute (Vol+) — +1 set on RDL progresses posterior chain volume; all other movements maintained to avoid excessive fatigue on the knee-adjacent tissues. 
      • WARMUP Glute bridges x20 + clamshells x15/side + hip circles 10min
      • WORK +1 set vs W1 — load can increase 2.5-5kg if W1 felt easy Romanian Deadlift (RDL) 5×8 RPE7
      • WORK Bump RPE — slightly heavier load Barbell Hip Thrust 4×10 RPE8
      • WORK Same depth constraint — can add light DBs if comfortable Bulgarian Split Squat (rear-foot elevated, shallow depth) 3×8 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 — continue 4s eccentric Nordic Hamstring Curl (eccentric focus) 3×7 RPE7
      • WORK Seated Calf Raise 3×15 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Supine hamstring stretch + hip flexor stretch 60s/side 5min
  Thu
    AM: [swim] Zone 2 Swim — Extended Aerobic — Thursday swim extends to 1800m total to progressively build aerobic base; effort stays Zone 2. 
      • WARMUP Easy choice stroke + 4x25m drills (catch-up, fingertip drag) 0.30km Z1
      • WORK Steady freestyle Zone 2 — bilateral breathing, long stroke 1.60km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Easy backstroke 0.10km Z1
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Upper Body B — Vertical Push/Pull (Vol+) — Mirroring Monday's volume increase on vertical patterns ensures balanced upper-body stimulus across the week. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + thoracic extension over foam roller + shoulder CARs 10min
      • WORK +1 set vs W1 — same load, chase same RPE Seated Dumbbell Overhead Press 5×8 RPE7
      • WORK +1 set vs W1 Lat Pulldown (wide grip) 5×10 RPE7
      • WORK Cable Lateral Raise 3×15 RPE7
      • WORK Face Pull (rope, cable) 3×15 RPE6
      • WORK Slight load increase EZ-Bar Curl 3×10 RPE8
      • WORK Triceps Pushdown (rope) 3×12 RPE8
      • COOLDOWN Cross-body shoulder stretch + pec minor stretch 60s/side 5min
  Sat
    AM: [bike] Long Zone 2 Bike — Extended (W2) — Extending the Saturday ride by 10 min follows the 10% rule and steadily builds aerobic capacity without additional knee stress. 
      • WARMUP Easy spin, gradually increase cadence to 85-90rpm 10min Z1 45% FTP
      • WORK Steady Zone 2 — high cadence (85-95rpm), nasal breathing 60min Z2 65% FTP
      • COOLDOWN Easy spin 5min Z1 40% FTP
  Sun (REST)
    (full rest)

── Week 3: Deload — volume cut ~35%, intensity preserved. 2 short quality sessions retained. Active recovery, tissue health focus. ──
  Mon
    AM: [strength] Upper Body A — Deload Push/Pull — Deload cuts sets to 3 on all compounds, allowing systemic recovery while keeping neuromuscular patterns grooved. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + arm circles + light cable row 10min
      • WORK Deload — same technique cues, reduce load ~10% Barbell Bench Press 3×8 RPE6
      • WORK Deload — control and feel, not effort Seated Cable Row (neutral grip) 3×10 RPE6
      • WORK Dumbbell Incline Press 2×10 RPE6
      • WORK Single-Arm Dumbbell Row 2×10 RPE6
      • WORK Overhead Triceps Extension (cable) 2×12 RPE6
      • WORK Dumbbell Hammer Curl 2×12 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Doorway chest stretch + lat hang 60s 5min
  Tue
    AM: [swim] Deload Zone 2 Swim — Easy Technique — Deload swim drops total distance ~35% and removes the tempo intervals to give the aerobic system a recovery stimulus. 
      • WARMUP Easy choice stroke 0.15km Z1
      • WORK Easy freestyle — focus on catch and pull mechanics, no effort push 0.80km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Easy backstroke 0.10km Z1
  Wed
    AM: [strength] Lower Body A — Deload Hip Hinge — Deload cuts lower-body sets by ~35%, enough stimulus to maintain the hip-hinge motor pattern without accumulating more fatigue. 
      • WARMUP Glute bridges x15 + clamshells x10/side + hip circles 8min
      • WORK Deload — reduce load ~10%, feel the hinge pattern Romanian Deadlift (RDL) 3×8 RPE6
      • WORK Deload — moderate load, full hip extension Barbell Hip Thrust 3×10 RPE6
      • WORK Bodyweight or light dumbbell only Bulgarian Split Squat (rear-foot elevated, shallow) 2×8 RPE6
      • WORK Maintain slow eccentric, fewer reps Nordic Hamstring Curl (eccentric focus) 2×5 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Supine hamstring stretch + hip flexor stretch 60s/side 5min
  Thu
    AM: [swim] Deload Zone 2 Swim — Easy Aerobic — Keeping the Thursday swim slot during deload maintains the training habit and pool access routine without adding meaningful fatigue. 
      • WARMUP Easy freestyle 0.15km Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 freestyle — relaxed effort, long stroke 1.00km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Easy backstroke 0.10km Z1
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Upper Body B — Deload Vertical Push/Pull — Friday deload mirrors Monday's volume reduction on vertical patterns; keeps shoulder overhead health work (face pulls) to continue tendon maintenance. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + thoracic extension + shoulder CARs 10min
      • WORK Deload — reduce load ~10% Seated Dumbbell Overhead Press 3×8 RPE6
      • WORK Deload — full range, slow pull Lat Pulldown (wide grip) 3×10 RPE6
      • WORK Cable Lateral Raise 2×15 RPE6
      • WORK Kept at 3 sets — shoulder health priority Face Pull (rope, cable) 3×15 RPE6
      • WORK EZ-Bar Curl 2×10 RPE6
      • WORK Triceps Pushdown (rope) 2×12 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Cross-body shoulder stretch + pec minor stretch 60s/side 5min
  Sat
    AM: [bike] Deload Zone 2 Bike — Short & Easy — Cutting bike duration by ~35% respects the deload mandate; Zone 2 intensity preserved to maintain aerobic adaptation. 
      • WARMUP Easy spin 8min Z1 45% FTP
      • WORK Zone 2 steady — high cadence (85-95rpm), nasal breathing 35min Z2 65% FTP
      • COOLDOWN Easy spin 5min Z1 40% FTP
  Sun (REST)
    (full rest)

scenarios.scenario_name: Athlete with overreaching markers — needs deload

constraint_adherence.reasoning: 6 days/week with 2 lifting days respected in both weeks (W1: Mon bike, Tue lift, Wed run, Fri lift, Sat run + 2 rest; W2: 6 sessions + 1 rest). Endurance emphasis preserved, no race constraints to violate, and the plan explicitly addresses the HRV/sleep/leg-heaviness red flags in the narrative and risks section.

periodization.reasoning: Week 1 functions as a true deload (~40% volume cut: runs shortened 11→8/10km, lifts cut to 2 sets, RPE 5-6), Week 2 reloads only ~10% with a conditional gate ('only if HRV >50 and sleep >6.5h'). Phase labeled base, deload cadence stated, and the plan correctly notes that if red flags persist Week 2 repeats Week 1.

intensity_distribution.reasoning: Week 1 is 100% Z1-Z2 with zero quality sessions — appropriate for the overreaching state. Week 2 introduces a single quality session (Thu bike tempo) deliberately placed on the bike to spare the legs, with Fri rest before Sat long run — proper hard/easy alternation. No heavy lower-body lift precedes a quality run (Fri lift in W1 → Sat easy run is at RPE 5-6, not heavy).

specificity.reasoning: Every session has concrete prescriptions: e.g., W1 Wed run '8.00km @ 5:35/km Z2', W1 Tue bench '2×8 RPE6.5', W2 Thu bike '3×8min Z3 78% FTP, 4min easy'. Pace derivation from 4.9 avg → 5:35 Z2 is shown. Each session includes a one-line rationale.

scenario_fit.reasoning: All must_haves met: W1 volume cut ~40%, zero quality sessions in W1, >85% easy (100% easy), W2 reload is conditional and conservative, narrative explicitly cites HRV 28% drop, 6.1h sleep, leg heaviness. No must_not_have violations: W1 long run is 10km (<15km), no RPE>7 lifting in W1 (capped at 6.5), no multiple quality sessions in W1.

strengths: Week 1 correctly treats the situation as mandatory recovery with zero quality work and explicit conditional gating for Week 2 reload (HRV >50, sleep >6.5h).
Quality reintroduction in W2 Thu is on the bike rather than run, intelligently protecting the legs that were reporting heaviness.
Risks section directly ties each prescription change to a specific recovery marker (HRV drop, sleep deficit, leg heaviness).
weaknesses: W2 Tue lift jumps from 2 sets RPE 6-6.5 to 3 sets RPE 7 plus added exercises — that's progressing volume, exercise selection, and intensity simultaneously, which violates the one-variable-at-a-time progression principle.
W2 Thu bike tempo at Z3 78% FTP is gray-zone work; a sharper threshold or sweet-spot prescription would be more adaptive if quality is being reintroduced at all.

scenarios.plan_summary:
Split: upper_lower
Narrative: ## Two-Week Plan: Recomp / Endurance Maintenance — Recovery-First Block

### Athlete Context
At 40yo with an advanced training history, this athlete is showing multiple simultaneous red flags that demand immediate action:
- **HRV crash: 42 vs 7-day avg of 58 — a 28% drop** (threshold for mandatory downshift is >15%)
- **Sleep averaging 6.1h** vs normal 7.5h (well below the 7h floor for quality work)
- **Leg heaviness + RPE inflation on easy runs** — classic accumulated fatigue signature

Per coaching protocol: **two or more simultaneous red flags = unconditional downshift.** No quality work until HRV recovers to within 10-15% of baseline (~50+) and sleep trends back toward 7h. These two weeks are structured as a **mandatory recovery block**, not a standard training block.

### Pace & Volume Calibration
- Avg easy run pace: 4.9 min/km → **Zone 2 target: ~5:25–5:40 min/km** (10-15% slower)
- Avg run distance: 11 km → **Long run target: 16–18 km** at easy pace (but deferred until Week 2 when recovery is established)
- Avg weekly runs: 5 → **Week 1: capped at 4 easy/short runs, no quality work**
- Lifting: 2x/week maintained but at RPE 6-7, volume reduced ~35%

### Week 1: Full Recovery Reset
All sessions are Zone 2 or below. No intervals, no threshold, no VO2max. Two lifting sessions at reduced volume (RPE 6-7). One full rest day. One active recovery/mobility day. Leg heaviness means lower-body lifting is dialed back significantly.

### Week 2: Cautious Reload
Assuming HRV has recovered toward 50+ and sleep improves to 6.5-7h, volume nudges up ~10%. One optional quality bike session (Zone 3 tempo — NOT a run, to protect the legs). Long run reintroduced at 16 km easy. If red flags persist entering Week 2, treat it as a second recovery week and repeat Week 1 structure.

### Split Type
Upper/Lower hybrid — 2 lifting days protecting lower body before running days, full-body accessible work.
Phase: base
Deload every: 3 weeks
Risks called out by planner: HRV is 28% below 7-day baseline with concurrent sleep deficit and physical symptoms — this meets the unconditional rest/downshift criterion. Any quality session this week risks tipping into overreaching/illness territory. | Sleep averaging 6.1h suppresses protein synthesis and hormonal recovery; even 'easy' sessions carry higher adaptive cost than normal. Volume must be reduced before compliance breaks down. | Leg heaviness on easy runs suggests either glycogen depletion, neuromuscular fatigue, or early overreaching — heavy lower-body lifts in Week 1 would compound this and should be avoided.

── Week 1: Mandatory recovery reset — all sessions Zone 1-2, volume cut ~40%, zero quality work, protect lower body from heavy loading ──
  Mon
    AM: [bike] Easy Z2 recovery ride — 35 min aerobic flush — Low-impact aerobic flush to stimulate blood flow and begin recovery without leg loading; cycling spares the run-specific musculature currently showing heaviness. 
      • WARMUP Spin up gradually 5min Z1 45% FTP
      • WORK Steady Zone 2 — fully conversational 30min Z2 58% FTP
      • COOLDOWN Easy spin out 5min Z1 40% FTP
  Tue
    AM: [strength] Upper body lift — horizontal push/pull, RPE 6-7 — Upper-body emphasis protects the recovering lower body; reduced volume (2 sets vs typical 3-4) and RPE cap at 7 maintains stimulus without CNS drain during HRV crash. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + shoulder CARs General upper-body activation 
      • WORK Barbell bench press Barbell bench press 2×8 RPE6.5
      • WORK Cable seated row Cable seated row 2×10 RPE6.5
      • WORK Dumbbell overhead press Dumbbell overhead press 2×10 RPE6
      • WORK Lat pulldown Lat pulldown 2×10 RPE6
      • WORK Dumbbell lateral raise Dumbbell lateral raise 2×15 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Thoracic mobility + pec/lat stretch 5min
  Wed
    AM: [run] Easy Z2 run — 8 km at 5:35/km, conversational — Shortened run (vs 11 km avg) at strictly enforced Zone 2 pace to maintain aerobic stimulus while respecting the fatigue and HRV crash; leg heaviness should inform willingness to slow further. 
      • WARMUP Walk + dynamic drills 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 — conversational, nasal breathing 8.00km @ 5:35/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + calf/hip flexor stretch 5min Z1
  Thu (REST)
    (full rest)
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Lower body lift — movement quality focus, RPE 6 — Maintaining lower-body movement patterns without adding to leg fatigue; 2 sets per movement at RPE 6 preserves neural patterns and blood flow without the tissue stress of heavy loading during an overreaching state. 
      • WARMUP Glute bridge + world's greatest stretch + leg swings Lower body activation circuit 
      • WORK Goblet squat — light, controlled tempo Goblet squat 2×10 @ 20kg RPE5
      • WORK Romanian deadlift — moderate load, feel the stretch Romanian deadlift 2×10 RPE6
      • WORK Bulgarian split squat — bodyweight only Bulgarian split squat 2×8 RPE5
      • WORK Seated calf raise Seated calf raise 2×15 RPE5
      • COOLDOWN Hip flexor stretch + foam roll quads/hamstrings 8min
  Sat
    AM: [run] Easy long run — 10 km at 5:35/km, Zone 2 only — Abbreviated long run (10 km vs normal 16-18 km target) to maintain aerobic base touch without deepening fatigue; HRV and sleep deficits make a true long run contraindicated this week. 
      • WARMUP 5 min easy walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Steady Zone 2 — nasal breathe, conversational 10.00km @ 5:35/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN 5 min walk + full-body stretch 5min Z1
  Sun (REST)
    (full rest)

── Week 2: Cautious reload — volume +10% from Week 1 only if HRV >50 and sleep >6.5h; reintroduce long run and one optional quality bike session ──
  Mon
    AM: [bike] Zone 2 bike — 45 min steady aerobic — Extending Monday bike session by 10 min vs Week 1 as a first cautious volume increase; cycling continues to protect run-specific leg musculature while building aerobic base. 
      • WARMUP Gradual spin-up 5min Z1 45% FTP
      • WORK Steady Zone 2 — conversational, controlled HR 40min Z2 62% FTP
      • COOLDOWN Easy spin out 5min Z1 40% FTP
  Tue
    AM: [strength] Upper body lift — push/pull + arms, RPE 7 — Progressive upper-body session adding one set to main compounds vs Week 1 and nudging RPE toward 7-7.5 as recovery markers improve; horizontal and vertical planes both covered. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + rotator cuff activation Upper body activation 
      • WORK Barbell bench press Barbell bench press 3×8 RPE7
      • WORK Barbell row (overhand) Barbell bent-over row 3×8 RPE7
      • WORK Dumbbell shoulder press Dumbbell overhead press 2×10 RPE7
      • WORK Lat pulldown Lat pulldown 2×10 RPE7
      • WORK Cable curl + tricep pushdown superset Cable bicep curl 2×12 RPE6.5
      • COOLDOWN Shoulder mobility + thoracic stretch 5min
  Wed
    AM: [run] Easy Z2 run — 10 km at 5:30/km, fully aerobic — Midweek easy run progressed from 8 km (Week 1) to 10 km; pace tightened marginally by 5 sec/km as recovery improves, still well within Zone 2 to protect Saturday's long run. 
      • WARMUP 5 min walk + dynamic leg swings 5min Z1
      • WORK Zone 2 — nasal breathe, could hold conversation 10.00km @ 5:30/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + hip flexor/calf stretch 5min Z1
  Thu
    AM: [bike] Bike tempo — 3x8 min @ Zone 3/FTP 78%, 4 min easy — First quality session reintroduced on the bike (not run) to avoid leg impact during reintroduction; Zone 3 tempo is one tier below threshold — meaningful stimulus with lower recovery cost, appropriate for a cautious Week 2 reload. 
      • WARMUP Easy spin + 3x20s leg openers 15min Z1 50% FTP
      • 3× (  WORK Tempo effort — Zone 3 8min Z3 78% FTP +   RECOVERY Easy spin recovery 4min Z1 50% FTP)
      • COOLDOWN Easy spin + stretch 10min Z1 45% FTP
  Fri (REST)
    (full rest)
  Sat
    AM: [run] Long run — 16 km at 5:30/km, Zone 2 — Full long run reintroduced in Week 2 after abbreviated version last week; 16 km is within the 1.5-2x avg distance guideline and consolidates the aerobic base with minimal injury risk at this pace and HR zone. 
      • WARMUP 8 min easy walk + dynamic drills 8min Z1
      • WORK Steady Zone 2 — hold conversation, check HR every 20 min 16.00km @ 5:30/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN 5 min walk + full body mobility 5min Z1
  Sun
    AM: [other] Active recovery — 30 min easy walk or mobility flow — Light active recovery 24h after long run promotes blood flow and reduces DOMS without adding training stress; keeps the 6-day week structure without overloading Sunday. 
      • WORK Easy walk or yoga/mobility — fully aerobic, RPE 2-3 30min Z1

scenarios.scenario_name: Masters athlete cutting weight while preserving muscle

constraint_adherence.reasoning: 4 lifting days (Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri) as requested, bike-only cardio matches preference, no running, squats Tue and deadlifts Fri (72h apart — not consecutive), bike sessions ≤30min satisfying the ≤45min cardio cap. No injury constraints to violate.

periodization.reasoning: Correctly applies 2:1 (2 loading + 1 deload) for masters athlete as specified. Week 3 deload cuts compound sets 5→3 and accessories 3→2 (~35-40% volume cut) while preserving RPE 8 and loads — textbook deload. Progression Week 1→2 uses single variable (one added compound set) keeping within 10% rule.

intensity_distribution.reasoning: All cardio is Zone 2 (60% FTP, 20-25 min work intervals) — no HIIT, no gray zone. Heavy lower work (Tue squat, Fri deadlift) never falls within 48h of each other. Bike sessions placed on upper days (Mon/Thu PM) to avoid leg interference. RPE 7-8 on compounds preserves strength without grinding.

specificity.reasoning: Every session specifies exercise×sets×reps×RPE (e.g., Wk2 Tue Back Squat 5×5 RPE8) and every bike session specifies duration + zone + %FTP (e.g., Wk1 Mon PM 20min Z2 60% FTP). Each session has a one-line rationale. Warmups and cooldowns included.

scenario_fit.reasoning: Heavy compounds preserved (Back Squat 4-5×5, Deadlift 4-5×5, Bench 4-5×6 at RPE 7-8). 2:1 loading explicitly applied. Working sets per muscle land in 12-15 range (e.g., quads ~13 sets/wk across Tue+Fri). Bike is Z2 ≤30min. Deload cuts ~35%. No HIIT, no >15-rep failure sets, no consecutive squat/dead, 4 lift days, no >45min endurance.

strengths: Exemplary deload execution in Week 3: volume cut ~35% (e.g., Back Squat 5×5→3×5) while RPE 8 and loads are preserved
Smart sequencing — Tue squat / Fri deadlift gives 72h separation, and bike sessions are tucked into upper-body days to avoid leg interference
Single-variable progressive overload (one added compound set Wk1→Wk2) respects the 10% rule and reduced recovery capacity
weaknesses: Week 2 Lower A has Back Squat 5×5 RPE8 plus RDL 3×8 RPE8 plus Leg Press 3×10 RPE8 — three RPE 8 lower compounds stacked is aggressive for a masters athlete in a deficit; one or two should stay at RPE 7
Total weekly cardio is modest (50-60 min Z2) — adequate for health but may underdeliver on the fat-loss caloric burn the cut goal implies
Lateral raises and face pulls dropped entirely in deload week — fine for volume reduction but no shoulder health maintenance work remains

scenarios.plan_summary:
Split: upper_lower
Narrative: **3-Week Strength-Emphasis Cut Block — 48yo Advanced Male**

**Athlete Context**
At 48, this athlete sits in the masters category, so we apply a **2:1 loading-to-deload ratio** (2 loading weeks + 1 deload) rather than the standard 3:1. Sleep averaging 6.9h is just below the 7h threshold — volume is trimmed ~10% preemptively to prevent compliance breakdown and manage recovery debt. HRV of 50 is moderate; watch for downward trends and autoregulate per protocol. Four lifting sessions per week with zero recent running is the baseline — bike cardio is introduced conservatively to support the cut without compromising strength output.

**Split Design: Upper / Lower (Hybrid)**
With 4 days available and a strength-emphasis cut goal, an Upper/Lower split delivers the best frequency-per-muscle-group (each muscle hit 2×/week) while leaving room for 2 short Zone 2 bike sessions to create the caloric deficit. Heavy lower-body lifts are never placed within 48h of each other. Bike sessions are kept short (25–35 min Zone 2) and placed on lower-intensity or off days to minimize interference.

**Phase: Accumulation → transitioning into Intensification**
- Week 1: Establish rhythm — RPE 7–8, moderate volume, calibrate loads
- Week 2: Progress volume +1 set on compounds, RPE 7–8 maintained, bike volume +5 min
- Week 3 (Deload): Volume cut ~35%, intensity preserved (same RPE, fewer sets), short bike sessions maintained

**Cardio Strategy**
No recent running history; bike is the tool for the cut. Zone 2 bike (conversational, ~65–70% max HR / FTP 55–65%) placed on Upper days or rest-adjacent days. Two sessions per week, 25–35 min, scaling up only in Week 2. This keeps the weekly cardio dose meaningful for fat loss without creating leg fatigue that bleeds into squat/deadlift days.

**Progressive Overload Plan**
- Week 1 → Week 2: Add 1 working set to the two main compounds per session; increase RPE target 7→8
- Week 2 → Week 3 (Deload): Drop back to Week 1 set counts, keep weights; reduce bike by ~30%

**Autoregulation Note**
Given 6.9h average sleep (borderline) and masters age: if HRV drops >10% below 7-day rolling average on any given day, downshift that session one tier (heavy → moderate, moderate → easy bike, easy bike → rest). Never grind through both a sleep <6h night AND low HRV.
Phase: base
Deload every: 3 weeks
Risks called out by planner: Sleep averaging 6.9h — borderline for recovery at 48yo; any further disruption should trigger a volume reduction of 10–15% that week or an early deload | Bike cardio on Upper days creates low interference risk, but if lower-body soreness is high after Week 2 Lower sessions, shift bike to rest days rather than stacking | Cut-phase caloric deficit + 4 lifting sessions risks muscle protein breakdown — protein intake should be ≥2.0g/kg (~172g/day) and a deload every 3rd week is non-negotiable

── Week 1: Establish rhythm — calibrate loads, moderate volume, RPE 7–8. Introduce Zone 2 bike to support cut. ──
  Mon
    AM: [strength] Upper A — Horizontal Push/Pull — Week 1 opener; horizontal emphasis to establish baseline loads on press and row patterns before adding vertical work mid-week. 
      • WARMUP General warm-up Band pull-aparts + arm circles + light cable rows

      • WORK Barbell Bent-Over Row Barbell Bent-Over Row 4×6 RPE7

      • WORK Tricep Pushdown Cable Tricep Pushdown 3×12 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Shoulder & chest stretch 5min
    PM: [bike] Zone 2 Bike — 25 min aerobic base — Short Zone 2 bike in PM adds caloric burn for the cut while keeping intensity low enough to not compromise Monday's lifting recovery. 
      • WARMUP Easy spin 5min Z1 45% FTP
      • WORK Steady Zone 2 20min Z2 60% FTP
      • COOLDOWN Easy spin down 5min Z1 40% FTP
  Tue
    AM: [strength] Lower A — Squat Emphasis — Squat pattern on Tuesday gives 48h before the next lower session on Thursday, satisfying the heavy compound separation rule. 
      • WARMUP Warm-up Goblet squat 2x10 + hip circles + leg swings

      • WORK Leg Press Leg Press 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Leg Curl Seated Leg Curl 3×12 RPE7
      • WORK Standing Calf Raise Standing Calf Raise 3×15 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Hip flexor & quad stretch 5min

    (full rest)

      • WORK Weighted Pull-Up Weighted Pull-Up 4×6 RPE7

      • WORK Face Pull Cable Face Pull 3×15 RPE7
      • WORK Barbell Curl Barbell Curl 3×12 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Lat & bicep stretch 5min
    PM: [bike] Zone 2 Bike — 25 min aerobic base — Second Zone 2 bike of the week on an upper-body lift day — no lower-body fatigue conflict, adds to weekly caloric deficit. 
      • WARMUP Easy spin 5min Z1 45% FTP
      • WORK Steady Zone 2 20min Z2 60% FTP
      • COOLDOWN Easy spin down 5min Z1 40% FTP
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Lower B — Deadlift Emphasis — Deadlift pattern on Friday is 72h removed from Tuesday squats and 48h before Saturday rest — optimal placement for a heavy hinge session.

── Week 2: Progress volume — add 1 working set to main compounds, RPE target 7→8. Bike sessions extended to 30 min. ──

    AM: [strength] Upper B — Vertical Push/Pull (Wk2) — Same overload mechanic as Upper A — one extra compound set at RPE 8. Vertical pattern remains distinct from Monday's horizontal emphasis.

    PM: [bike] Zone 2 Bike — 30 min aerobic base — Second weekly Zone 2 bike extended to 30 min; upper-body lift day means no lower-body fatigue conflict. 
      • WARMUP Easy spin 5min Z1 45% FTP

      • COOLDOWN Easy spin down 5min Z1 40% FTP
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Lower B — Deadlift Emphasis (Wk2) — Extra deadlift set at RPE 8 continues the progressive overload theme — the single hardest session of the week demands no bike interference on Friday. 
      • WARMUP Warm-up Hip hinge drill + glute bridge 2x12 + light deadlift warm-up sets 
      • WORK Conventional Deadlift Conventional Deadlift 5×5 RPE8
